warps.notes: Staircase up to 6F — south-central area of floor

notes: 5F has one visible item ball (Rare Candy at 9,9) and two hidden items.
Hidden: Full Restore at (16,14), Carbos at (3,15).
Three return warps to 4F at (2,4), (2,14), (17,15) — mirror of 4F's upward warps.
The Rare Candy is the most valuable openly visible item in the tower climb.

items.name: Rare Candy
items.notes: Item ball (SPRITE_POKE_BALL). Script: TinTower5FRareCandy — itemball RARE_CANDY. Centrally placed.

items.name: Full Restore
items.notes: Hidden item (bg_event). Script: TinTower5FHiddenFullRestore — hiddenitem FULL_RESTORE.

items.name: Carbos
items.notes: Hidden item (bg_event). Script: TinTower5FHiddenCarbos — hiddenitem CARBOS.

access_requirements.to_enter: Reached via any of three warps from 4F: warp_id 1 (2,4), warp_id 3 (2,14), or warp_id 4 (17,15).
access_requirements.note: Multiple 4F-to-5F entry points create the Tin Tower's signature warp puzzle — players must find the correct paths by exploring both floors.

notes: Tin Tower 5F is 10x9 blocks (20x18 tiles). It is the midpoint of the nine-floor tower. The floor contains one visible item ball (Rare Candy at 9,9) and two hidden items (Full Restore at 16,14; Carbos at 3,15 via bg_events). The warp structure mirrors 4F: three return-warps at (2,4), (2,14), and (17,15) connect back down to 4F, while the sole ascending stair at (11,15) leads to 6F. The interlinked 4F/5F warp system forms the puzzle core of the lower tower — players dropped to wrong 5F positions may need to re-explore 4F to find the upward path. No NPCs or trainers are present on this floor.
